en.extract:
Wallace's owlet-nightjar (Aegotheles wallacii) is a species of bird in the family Aegothelidae. It is found in New Guinea.
It is named after Alfred Russel Wallace, a British naturalist, explorer, geographer, and biologist.

'''Wallace's owlet-nightjar''' ('''''Aegotheles wallacii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Aegothelidae]]. It is found in [[New Guinea]].

It is named after [[Alfred Russel Wallace]], a British [[naturalist]], explorer, geographer, and biologist.<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|pages=357–358|author2=Watkins, Michael }}</ref>
